title: Proofpoint TAP Phishing Classification Volume by Sender Domain
app: Proofpoint TAP Modular Input for Splunk (Splunkbase 3681)
spl:
index=email sourcetype="proofpoint_tap_siem" earliest=-7d
| eval cls=coalesce(classification, Classification, "")
| where cls="Phish"
| eval from_addr=coalesce(sender, Sender, fromAddress, FromAddress, "")
| eval dom=replace(from_addr, "^[^@]+@", "")
| stats count by dom
| sort -count
description: Aggregating Proofpoint TAP events classified as Phish by sending domain highlights persistent phishing infrastructure, compromised senders, and bulk campaigns targeting the tenant.
value: Turns TAP’s condemnation metadata into domain-level situational awareness for threat intel feeds, firewall blocks, and executive reporting without exporting CSVs from Proofpoint.
implementation: Ensure `classification` is extracted; multi-threat messages may list multiple classes—use `mvexpand` on `threatsInfoMap` if counts look low. Normalize `sender` formats (envelope vs header). Join top domains to WHOIS or intel lookups. Schedule daily with adaptive thresholds for seasonal spikes.
visualization: Bar chart (top domains), pie (internal vs external domains), timechart of phish events.

references.title: Proofpoint TAP Modular Input for Splunk
